items.title: Claude Opus 4.7 - Anthropic
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7 in April 2026, marking its latest flagship model update. The release features improved software engineering capabilities, higher-resolution vision processing, and adaptive thinking that automatically calibrates reasoning depth based on task complexity. It also introduces new effort controls, task budgets, and Claude Code review tools. Pricing starts at $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens, with up to 90% savings via prompt caching. Nota

items.title: Claude Platform API Release Notes Overview
items.author: platform.claude.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has shipped several significant API updates in early 2026. The most notable new release is Claude Opus 4.7, the most capable generally available model for complex reasoning and agentic coding, priced at the same $5/$25 per million tokens as Opus 4.6 but with API breaking changes. Claude Managed Agents launched in public beta — a fully managed agent harness with secure sandboxing, built-in tools, and server-sent event streaming, requiring the managed-agents-2026-04-01 beta header. The a

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, a new product for creating quick visuals | TechCrunch
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: Anthropic's @AnthropicAI account has been active with several major announcements in April 2026. On April 17, Anthropic launched Claude Design, a new experimental product enabling users to create quick visuals including prototypes, slides, and one-pagers directly through Claude. On April 16, Claude Opus 4.7 was released as the most capable publicly available model, featuring major coding benchmark gains (64.3% on SWE-bench Pro), a new 'xhigh' reasoning effort level, tripled image resolution supp

items.title: Advancing Claude in Healthcare and Life Sciences
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has made several major announcements in 2026. Most notably, they previewed Claude Mythos, a new general-purpose model with exceptional capabilities in computer security tasks — prompting the launch of Project Glasswing, an initiative to use Mythos to help secure critical software. Anthropic also launched Claude Sonnet 4.6, a balanced model with improved agentic search performance and lower token consumption, along with Claude Managed Agents entering public beta — a fully managed agent

items.title: The Hot Mess of AI — Alignment Blog
items.author: alignment.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has published several notable research papers and reports in early 2026. On the AI safety front, researchers released 'The Hot Mess of AI,' which decomposes errors of frontier reasoning models into bias and variance components, finding that as tasks increase in difficulty, model failures become dominated by incoherence rather than systematic misalignment. A companion paper on 'Natural Emergent Misalignment from Reward Hacking in Production RL' examines how reward hacking during reinfor
